# vnfin

Clean-room, no-key-first Python library for Vietnam financial-market data: equity prices,
fundamentals, mutual funds, indices, gold, FX, crypto, and macro indicators. Results are typed and
carry explicit units. Most domains use same-unit failover where clean backups exist.

Install: `pip install git+https://github.com/hungson175/vnfin.git` (add `[pandas]` for
`.to_dataframe()`). Python >= 3.10.

## Human documentation path

- [README](README.md): primary human entry point — install, quickstart, common jobs.
- [Docs index](docs/index.md): progressive-disclosure docs home.
- [Getting started](docs/getting-started.md): 10-minute walkthrough.
- Tutorials: [stock prices](docs/tutorials/stock-prices.md),
  [fundamentals](docs/tutorials/fundamentals.md),
  [funds and indices](docs/tutorials/funds-and-indices.md),
  [macro and FX](docs/tutorials/macro-and-fx.md),
  [historical FX](docs/tutorials/fx-history.md),
  [gold and crypto](docs/tutorials/gold-and-crypto.md).
- How-to: [pandas DataFrames](docs/how-to/pandas-dataframes.md),
  [errors](docs/how-to/errors.md), [cache/retry](docs/how-to/cache-retry.md),
  [FRED BYOK](docs/how-to/byok-fred.md), [live tests](docs/how-to/live-tests.md).
- Reference: [public API](docs/api.md), [units](docs/units.md),
  [stability](docs/stability.md), [sources](docs/sources/).

## AI-agent material

- [AI usage guide](docs/ai-usage.md): comprehensive copy-paste reference for agents.
- [vnfin skill](skills/vnfin/SKILL.md): Anthropic-style skill with per-domain reference.

## Non-negotiable usage rules

1. No key is needed for default paths. Optional BYOK: `FRED_API_KEY`; optional gold token override:
   `VNFIN_BTMC_WIDGET_KEY`.
2. `client()` means failover chain and `source()` means primary single source, except `gold` (use
   `vn()` / `world()` because units differ).
3. Always read units from result objects: equities VND, indices points, fundamentals raw VND or
   per-line ratio units, FX VND per 1 base, gold VND/lượng or USD/oz.
4. History calls require valid `start`/`end` dates unless a specific API says otherwise.
5. `.to_dataframe()` requires the pandas extra.
